takes.event_basis: <cite index="23-1">NVDA's next earnings date is CONFIRMED for Wednesday 05/20/2026 After Market</cite>. <cite index="21-17">Wall Street is looking for $1.77 in EPS and a 78% year-over-year increase in revenue</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="21-3,21-14">NVIDIA's quarterly results usually beat analysts' consensus expectations, with a history of earning beats</cite>. <cite index="26-1">CEO Jensen Huang participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; combined with strong hyperscaler capex ($725B+ annually), Blackwell demand should exceed guidance</cite>. Margin resilience and 2026-27 $1T processor roadmap provide upside optionality.
takes.risks: Valuation extension post-beat; softening forward guidance on H200/Rubin demand; geopolitical China export volatility despite summit.

takes.event_basis: Q1 FY27 Earnings on 2026-05-22 (after market close). CEO Huang's participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; hyperscaler capex spending on track for $725B+ combined annually.
takes.thesis: <cite index="31-8">CEO Huang's participation in US delegation summit with China suggests potential pathway for advanced chip exports, removing regulatory headwinds on Chinese demand.</cite> Combined with sustained AI capex momentum and <cite index="6-9">hyperscaler combined 2026 spending projections tracking near $725 billion, above earlier expectations,</cite> NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and raised guidance.
takes.risks: Geopolitical detente could reverse if US-Iran tensions escalate; inflation surprise may prompt Warsh Fed to signal rate-hold policy extending into H2 2026.

takes.event_basis: <cite index="32-11">Intensive talks between the two companies have been ongoing for more than a year, and they hammered out a formal deal in recent months</cite>. Formal contract execution and production roadmap for Apple deal expected Q2–Q3 2026.
takes.thesis: <cite index="33-2,33-4">The deal would be the most notable vote of confidence yet for Intel's once-struggling chip foundry business</cite>. <cite index="2-1,2-18">The foundry is now expected to break even in 2027, thanks to deals with Amazon, Microsoft, the U.S. Government, and Tesla</cite>. Apple production ramp (likely 2027+) validates 18A process and justifies Trump admin backing—de-risks foundry margin recovery thesis.
takes.risks: Qualification delays (2–3 years typical); execution risk on 18A yields; tariff headwinds; Apple could pivot to Samsung if yields slip.
